 {"id":"bd-2q98","title":"Add bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and rich error types","description":"Implement Bubbletea Println/Printf unmanaged output and richer error types to match Go ergonomics.

Scope:
- Add Program-level Println/Printf helpers that write outside the TUI renderer.
- Ensure output respects raw/alt-screen modes and restores terminal appropriately.
- Define richer error types for program failures (I/O, renderer, terminal).
- Add unit tests and integration tests for unmanaged output.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Println/Printf APIs documented and tested in both sync/async Program modes.
- Rich error types carry context and are surfaced to callers.
- Integration tests show unmanaged output without corrupting screen.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/error.rs (if present), tests in crates/bubbletea or tests/conformance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:33.022679833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639129033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:54:15.639065976Z","close_reason":"Completed: println/printf commands and rich error types are fully implemented. Commands export from crate root, handle alt-screen mode, unit tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2q9t","title":"Fix flaky snapshot tests: Duration values change with wall-clock time","description":"## Problem
The snapshot tests for jobs page (snapshot_jobs_80x24, snapshot_jobs_120x40) are flaky because the Duration field is calculated from real wall-clock time against old started_at timestamps.

Each test run shows a different duration (e.g., '19371h 34m' → '19371h 36m') causing repeated failures.

## Root Cause
In crates/demo_showcase/src/data/generator.rs, jobs are created with started_at timestamps that are far in the past. The Duration display calculates Utc::now() - started_at, which changes every minute.

## Fix Options
1. Use insta snapshot filters to ignore Duration field
2. Use deterministic mock time in tests  
3. Modify test data to use fixed/relative timestamps

## Acceptance
- Snapshot tests pass consistently without needing cargo insta accept
- Duration display still works correctly in production","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"StormyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-29T03:43:51.198611987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T03:46:25.335906253Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T03:46:25.335837875Z","close_reason":"Fixed by adding regex-based Duration redaction in snapshot_view(). Snapshots now use '[REDACTED]' for time-based Duration values, making tests deterministic.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-2qjc","title":"Unit Tests: charmed_log formatting","description":"Unit tests for charmed_log logging framework.

## Current State
charmed_log has 179 lines of tests - very shallow for a logger.

## Test Requirements

### Log Formatting
- [ ] timestamp formats (RFC3339, custom)
- [ ] log level formatting
- [ ] message formatting
- [ ] structured field formatting
- [ ] caller information

### Level Filtering
- [ ] minimum level enforcement
- [ ] per-module level overrides
- [ ] runtime level changes

### Output Writers
- [ ] stdout/stderr selection
- [ ] file output
- [ ] custom writer support
- [ ] buffering behavior

### Styles
- [ ] color schemes by level
- [ ] custom color overrides
- [ ] no-color mode
- [ ] style with lipgloss integration

### Structured Logging
- [ ] key-value pairs
- [ ] nested structures
- [ ] array values
- [ ] special characters in values

### Edge Cases
- [ ] very long messages
- [ ] high-frequency logging
- [ ] concurrent logging
- [ ] newlines in messages

## Success Criteria
- All format options tested
- Thread safety verified
- Performance acceptable under load","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:52:57.710366058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:01:22.773017056Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:01:22.772888106Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qjc","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2qou","title":"lipgloss ThemeContext: recover from poisoned RwLock instead of panicking","description":"ThemeContext currently uses expect("...lock poisoned") for current/listeners locks, which can panic and take down runtime after a prior panic poisons the lock. Replace with poison-recovery helpers (into_inner) and add regression tests for poisoned current/listeners locks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:31.113765615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:51.623577263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:30:51.623511331Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qq7","title":"Wizard: integrate with demo data + trigger jobs/deployments","description":"Wire wizard completion into the demo backend.

- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so the Wizard and Jobs page don't depend on each other.

Examples:

- Emits log lines and toasts.

- Behavior is covered by unit tests at the action-API layer.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:03.497458412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:28:55.427272909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:28:55.427150902Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","demo_showcase","forms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-2omz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qq7","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qx1","title":"demo_showcase page: Logs (styled + filterable)","description":"A live log viewer page that feels like a production-grade log tailer.

Why:
- Logging is where real apps live and die; this is the most convincing place to show `charmed_log` styling + ergonomics.
- It also provides the “ground truth” for cross-page workflows: Jobs should link to logs; errors should explain themselves.

Must showcase:
- `charmed_log`-styled output:
  - level coloring (trace/debug/info/warn/error)
  - structured fields (service, job_id, request_id, duration, user)
  - readable formatting even at narrow widths
- Bubbles viewport:
  - smooth scrolling, jump to top/bottom
  - follow mode (“tail -f”) that pauses when the user scrolls up
  - optional line wrapping toggle
- Filtering/search:
  - filter by level and service
  - query search with match highlight + next/prev navigation
  - correlation filters (e.g., “show logs for selected job”)
- Actions:
  - copy selected range or last N lines
  - export to a file (with a clear artifact path) and show toast confirmation
  - clear/reset view (without breaking follow mode)

Data requirements:
- Log stream is deterministic in test mode and correlated with the Jobs simulation.
- Includes occasional warnings/errors so filtering and drill-down are meaningful.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a real log tailer (usable, not flashy-for-flashy’s-sake).
- Filtering is instant and predictable; follow mode never fights the user.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filter + search correctness (stable ordering, edge cases)
  - follow mode logic (scroll up pauses, scroll bottom resumes)
  - export/copy behavior produces expected artifacts/messages
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - logs copy/export/clear scenario (verifies artifacts + JSONL logs)

Notes:
- Avoid “fake rainbow logs”; prioritize legibility and density.
- Ensure `--no-color` / ASCII modes still produce clear output.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.489282051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:17:42.678651544Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:17:42.678509840Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rlk","title":"Replace assert!(false) fallback in ssh_e2e bad-password flow","description":"In crates/demo_showcase/tests/ssh_e2e.rs, remove the synthetic assert!(false, ...) fallback in the bad-password test command-spawn branch and replace it with a direct assertion on Result plus explicit early return for type flow. Keeps test failure semantics while reducing code smell.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:43:35.192028874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:44:21.504226780Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:44:21.504162761Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
